message:
fix(process): close piped stdin before wait/async_wait to prevent child deadlock

Both wait() and async_wait() now drop(self.stdin.take()) before
entering their respective blocking/polling loops.  This matches
std::process::Child::wait semantics: closing the parent's write
end of the stdin pipe sends EOF to the child, allowing programs
like `cat` (which read until EOF) to terminate instead of
deadlocking the wait.

Without this fix, a child process with Stdio::Pipe stdin would
block forever reading from the still-open pipe while the parent
blocked forever waiting for the child to exit.

Test: test_wait_closes_piped_stdin_before_blocking spawns `cat`
with piped stdin, calls wait() without explicitly closing stdin,
and verifies the child exits with status 0 within a timeout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
